zotero.abstractNote: We propose a new approach to train provably robust boosted decision stumps and trees against adversarial attacks. Our method is based on a robust version of the exponential loss which allows us to derive a tight upper bound on the adversarial loss. We show that for decision stumps, the robust loss can be minimized efficiently. For decision trees, we propose a greedy training algorithm that minimizes the robust loss at each split. Our approach provides a certificate of robustness for the final model, meaning that for any input, we can guarantee that the prediction does not change within a given perturbation set. We evaluate our method on several datasets and show that it achieves state-of-the-art robust accuracy for tree-based models, while being significantly faster to train than existing methods.
